name: Encore (Backend Framework with Built-in Infra)
pricing_notes: Open source (Mozilla 2.0); Encore Cloud is commercial (managed platform)

auth.notes: Encore Cloud uses API key; self-hosted via Docker/Kubernetes

performance.notes: Optimized runtime; Go-based framework highly performant; TS/JS version also available

agent_readiness.notes: Backend framework where infrastructure is inferred from code. encore.service(), api.get(), pubsub.Topic(), cache.Keyspace(), sqldb.Named(). Framework generates Terraform/Pulumi/K8s from service definitions. TypeScript and Go support. Local dev with encore run. Encore Cloud for managed deployment to AWS/GCP/Azure. Auto-generates API docs, architecture diagrams, request explorer. Distributed tracing built in. Microservices with service-to-service type safety. Unique approach: infra as code annotations in business logic.

gotchas: Framework lock-in — non-standard approach to infra
Pre-1.0 TS SDK — Go SDK more mature
Encore Cloud required for advanced features
best_when: Greenfield TypeScript or Go microservices backends
Teams wanting infrastructure auto-generated from application code
Startups wanting opinionated full-stack backend without DevOps overhead
Projects valuing built-in distributed tracing and API docs
summary: Encore is a backend framework where infrastructure is inferred from code annotations — define services, APIs, pub/sub, caches, and databases in code; Encore generates the deployment infrastructure. TypeScript and Go support. Encore Cloud for managed AWS/GCP/Azure deployment. Built-in distributed tracing, API docs, architecture diagrams. Best for greenfield teams wanting opinionated full-stack backend without manual infrastructure management.
